commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f4a1239a49cc9e36b3f368debd9d113561ec3744)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a `repair:pr-intake` CLI that scans an author's open PRs for repair signals, writes `repair-pr-` jobs, preserves that job prefix during state publishes, and removes `--ephemeral` from repair Codex subprocess calls.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a current-main bug report, but the PR findings are source-reproducible: the added intake code includes `CANCELLED` as a failed signal and emits `repair_cluster` job intent for a single-PR repair job.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer/product review plus code fixes for repair signal selection and PR-repair job routing; the durable intent semantics are not a safe automated repair choice yet.

target.title: Add repair-only PR intake
target.body:
## Summary
- add deterministic repair-only PR intake for open PRs with current objective repair signals
- create repair jobs from failed checks, dirty merge state, changes requested, or unresolved current review threads
- teach repair workers to inspect source PR refs instead of treating source PRs as canonical material
- preserve generated repair-pr jobs during broad jobs state publishes
- avoid treating transient GitHub UNKNOWN mergeability as an actionable repair signal by itself

## Pre-publication checks
- checked upstream open PRs before publishing; no existing Jhacarreiro-authored PR covers repair-only intake
- compared open relevant upstream PRs (#250, #251, #253, #274, #281, #164); only file overlap was package.json with #281, both adding different scripts
- rebased on current upstream/main a1f510381f
- sanitized diff for local-only names, paths, hosts, private services, and secrets

## Real behavior proof

Dry-run against real upstream PR data after the first ClawSweeper review on this PR:

    corepack pnpm run repair:pr-intake -- --repo openclaw/clawsweeper --author Jhacarreiro --dry-run --limit 20 --include-review-comments-only

Output excerpt:

    {
      "status": "ok",
      "repo": "openclaw/clawsweeper",
      "author": "Jhacarreiro",
      "scanned": 1,
      "candidates": 1,
      "dry_run": true,
      "jobs": [
        {
          "status": "planned",
          "job": "jobs/openclaw/inbox/repair-pr-openclaw-clawsweeper-290.md",
          "number": 290,
          "signals": [
            { "kind": "check_failed", "detail": "notify: conclusion=CANCELLED" },
            { "kind": "comment_actionable", "source": "https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/pull/290#issuecomment-4707255583" }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }

This would have prevented exactly the first failed submission of this PR from sitting unhandled: the intake detects the ClawSweeper failure/comment, plans a durable repair-pr job, and does so in dry-run without mutating queue state.

## Verification
- pnpm run build:repair
- node --test test/repair/git-publish.test.ts test/repair/run-worker.test.ts
- node --test test/repair/run-worker.test.ts
target.author: jhacarreiro

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
